description: Chrysophyllum argenteum is a tree in the family Sapotaceae, native to the tropical Americas.
origin: LEE MO, PUE, TRT, SUR OO, SUR

care.tips: Native to: CARIBBEAN, BZN RO, BZN PA, VEN, CUB

description: Chrysophyllum cainito is a tropical tree of the family Sapotaceae. It is native to the Isthmus of Panama, where it was domesticated. It has spread to the Greater Antilles and the West Indies and is now grown throughout the tropics, including Southeast Asia. It grows rapidly and reaches 20 meters in height.
origin: MEXICO, QLD QU, NIC, DOM OO, WIN DO
synonyms: ["Chrysophyllum sericeum", "Chrysophyllum cainito var. caeruleum", "Chrysophyllum cainito var. martinicense", "Chrysophyllum bonplandii", "Chrysophyllum caeruleum", "Chrysophyllum cainito var. jamaicense", "Chrysophyllum cainito var. typicum", "Chrysophyllum jamaicense", "Chrysophyllum cainito var. pomiferum", "Chrysophyllum maliforme"]

care.tips: Native to: CARIBBEAN, BLZ, CUB, VEN, SWC NC

description: Chrysophyllum flexuosum is a tree species in the family Sapotaceae found in Brazil.
origin: BZL ES, BZL MG, BZE PE, BZL TR, BZL SP
synonyms: ["Chrysophyllum flexuosum var. majus", "Chrysophyllum elegans", "Villocuspis flexuosa", "Chrysophyllum flexuosum var. cuspidatum", "Chrysophyllum lanceolatum", "Villocuspis elegans"]

care.tips: Native to: BZL ES, BZE MA, BZL, BZL SP, BZE BA

description: Chrysophyllum gonocarpum is a tree in the family Sapotaceae, native to South America.
Chrysophyllum marginatum is a tree in the family Sapotaceae, native to South America.
origin: BZC MS, BZE AL, BOL, AGW CA, BZC GO

care.tips: Native to: BZC DF, BZC GO, AGW SJ, BZE PB, AGE FO

description: Chrysophyllum mexicanum is a plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to Mexico and Central America.
origin: MEXICO, MXE ZA, NIC, MXC TL, MXC ME
synonyms: ["Cynodendron mexicanum", "Micropholis sericea", "Chrysophyllum mexicanum var. politum", "Chrysophyllum mexicanum var. typicum"]

care.tips: Native to: BLZ, MXS, MXE AG, MXT QR, MXS MI

description: Chrysophyllum oliviforme, commonly known as the satinleaf, is a medium-sized tree native to Florida, the Bahamas, the Greater Antilles, and Belize. It is also known as damson plum, wild star-apple and saffron-tree. It gets the name "satinleaf" from the distinctive colors of the leaves. The top of the leaf is dark green while the bottom is light brown or copper. This distinctive look makes it a very aesthetically pleasing tree that is commonly used as an ornamental in yards and public spaces.
origin: BAH, MXE AG, LEE MO, PUE, HAW MI
synonyms: ["Guersentia oliviformis", "Dactimala oliviformis", "Cynodendron oliviforme", "Chrysophyllum oliviforme var. typicum"]

care.tips: Native to: MLY, SWC HC, LEE BV, WIN GR, ASIA TROPICAL

description: Labourdonnaisia calophylloides is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae native to the islands of Mauritius and Réunion in the Indian Ocean.
origin: REU, MAU, WESTERN INDIAN OCEAN, AFRICA, MAU OO

description: Manilkara bidentata is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to a large area of northern South America, Central America and the Caribbean. Common names include bulletwood, balatá, ausubo, massaranduba, quinilla, and (ambiguously) "cow-tree".
origin: BOL, BZN AM, DOM OO, WIN DO, TRT OO

description: Manilkara obovata is an evergreen tree in the Sapotaceae family. Its timber is sold under the name Nkunya in Uganda. The species has a wide distribution from Sierra Leone in West Africa moving east to Uganda in Eastern Africa and southwards to Zambia. It is also considered a variable species having different ecotypes.
origin: WEST CENTRAL TROPICAL AFRICA, BEN OO, MLI, GGI AN, GUI OO
synonyms: ["Mimusops densiflora", "Mimusops atacorensis", "Manilkara multinervis subsp. atacorensis", "Manilkara multinervis subsp. argentea", "Manilkara maclaudii", "Manilkara densiflora", "Manilkara pobeguinii", "Mimusops chevalieri", "Mimusops cuneifolia", "Manilkara angolensis"]

origin: BZL TR, BZN AM, BZE CE, BZE PB, BZN TO

description: Manilkara subsericea is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is endemic to Brazil, and threatened by habitat loss.
origin: BZL, BZL ES, BZL MG, BZS RS, BZS PR
synonyms: ["Kaukenia floribunda", "Manilkara bella", "Synarrhena subsericea", "Synarrhena floribunda", "Mimusops floribunda", "Mimusops subsericea var. massaranduba", "Mimusops subsericea var. acuminata", "Mimusops subsericea", "Mimusops subsericea var. acmanthera", "Kaukenia subsericea"]

description: Manilkara zapota, commonly known as sapodilla, sapote, chicozapote, chico, chicoo, chicle, naseberry, nispero, or soapapple, among other names, is an evergreen tree native to southern Mexico and Central America. An example natural occurrence is in coastal Yucatán, in the Petenes mangroves ecoregion, where it is a subdominant plant species. It was introduced to the Philippines during Spanish colonization.
origin: NIC, MXC TL, VAN, TRT OO, CUB
synonyms: ["Achras coriacea", "Lucuma mammosa", "Achras dactylina", "Mimusops grisebachii", "Lucuma zapota var. anguai", "Sapota achras var. sphaerica", "Achras sapota", "Manilkara achras", "Achras tchicomame", "Achras paludosa"]

description: Mimusops balata is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to Mauritius and Réunion.

Mimusops elengi is a medium-sized evergreen tree found in tropical forests in South Asia, Southeast Asia and northern Australia. English common names include Spanish cherry, medlar, and bullet wood. Its timber is valuable and its fruit is edible. As the trees give thick shade and flowers emit fragrance, it is a prized collection for gardens. It is used as an ornamental tree in many places. The flowers may also be used in natural perfume.
Niemeyera whitei known as the rusty plum or plum boxwood is a species of tree native to eastern Australia.

Planchonella australis, also known by the synonym Pouteria australis, is a medium to tall rainforest tree of the family Sapotaceae native to Queensland and New South Wales, Australia. It is known by the common name black apple, wild plum, yellow buttonwood, black plum and yellow bulletwood.

Planchonella obovata is a species of tree in the family Sapotaceae. The common name in Australia is the northern yellow boxwood. It occurs in many parts of south-east Asia, Micronesia, and on islands of the Indian Ocean, and has local common names there.

Pouteria caimito, the abiu, is a tropical fruit tree in the family Sapotaceae. It grows in the Amazonian region of South America, and this type of fruit can also be found in Cuba, the Philippines and other countries in Southeast Asia. It grows to an average of 10 metres high, with ovoid fruits. The inside of the fruit is translucent and white. It has a creamy and jelly-like texture with a taste resembling caramel custard.†

Pouteria campechiana is an evergreen tree native to, and cultivated in, southern Mexico, Belize, Guatemala, and El Salvador. It is cultivated in other countries, such as India, Costa Rica, Brazil, the United States, the Dominican Republic, Australia, Cambodia, Vietnam, Taiwan, Indonesia, Sri Lanka, Nigeria, Cameroon and the Philippines. The edible part of the tree is its fruit, which is colloquially known as an egg fruit.

Pouteria glomerata is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is distributed from Mexico to North-East Argentina. Its greatest presence is in Brazil, where it is known as abiurana-do-igapó. Mature fruit has a smooth and yellow pericarp, with four ovary locules. The subspecies Pouteria glomerata subsp. stylosa is endemic to the Amazon Basin, where it is called abiurana-roxa. The subspecies Pouteria glomerata subsp.
Pouteria torta is a species of tree native to Central and South America. It is found largely in the Brazilian cerrado and is commonly called guapeva and grão-de-galo.

Sersalisia sericea, also known as wild prune, mangarr, and mongo is a species of shrubs or small trees, of the plant family Sapotaceae They grow naturally in monsoon forest, littoral rainforest, and occasionally in more open forest types across Northern Australia.
1/2  long slender shiny globular black fruits mature in the fall with a single seed inside. Occurs singly or in groups of 2-3.
origin: South central U.S.A. and Mexico

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) The Kiowa and Comanche tribes use the fruit for food and a substance in the outer bark as chewing gum.
care.edible_parts: Fruit is edible but should be eaten in small quantities to prevent belly aches.

description: Vitellaria paradoxa, commonly known as shea tree, shi tree, or vitellaria, is a tree of the family Sapotaceae. It is the only species in the genus Vitellaria, and is indigenous to Africa.
